title: [SERA_VNEXT_HUMAN_PILOT] Practice easy clean escape
narrative: The aircraft was unstable at the published gate. The pilot monitoring called unstable and the pilot flying continued below the gate before a later go-around.
what_to_observe: First useful go-around opportunity is before the later recovery
expected_reviewer_attention: Confirm actor and escape point without using the outcome as cause

title: [SERA_VNEXT_HUMAN_PILOT] Practice ambiguous automation
narrative: The crew expected the automation to capture a descent restriction. The mode did not change and the aircraft drifted above path before correction.
what_to_observe: Mode awareness may or may not be supported
expected_reviewer_attention: Look for explicit awareness and feedback evidence

title: [SERA_VNEXT_HUMAN_PILOT] Practice evidence insufficient
narrative: A short report says the aircraft deviated from heading and returned. It does not identify the flying pilot callouts automation mode or cues before the deviation.

title: [SERA_VNEXT_HUMAN_PILOT] Practice consequence trap
narrative: The report emphasizes a runway excursion. Earlier facts show the aircraft crossed the threshold high and fast with a safe go-around still available.
what_to_observe: Outcome is later than the escape point
expected_reviewer_attention: Keep causal review anchored before the consequence

title: [SERA_VNEXT_HUMAN_PILOT] Practice strong precondition
narrative: High workload and fatigue were documented. The crew still had a clear point to stop and rebrief after a late runway change but continued taxi.
what_to_observe: Fatigue is context not the unsafe act
expected_reviewer_attention: Record precondition without replacing the direct actor decision
